rulings.Case Title: CALIFORNIA UNION SQUARE L.P. VS. SAKS & COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Appoint Arbitrator Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 1281.6
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 22, 2018, Line 1. PETITIONER CALIFORNIA UNION SQUARE L.P.'S Motion To Appoint Arbitrator Pursuant To Cal. Code Civ. Proc. Section 1281.6. Continued to May 17, 2018. By no later than April 27, 2018 by 4: 00 p.m. each party shall file a list of 5 proposed arbitrators accompanied by CVs, and a brief not to exceed 3 pages addressing why each proposed arbitrator is qualified. Each proposed arbitrator shall be ready and willing to conduct the arbitration. By no later than May 7, 2018 parties are to meet and confer regarding each of the 10 proposed arbitrators. By no later than May 14, 2018 parties shall file a joint brief specifying objections to each proposed arbitrator, if any, and response to the objection. The joint brief shall be in a table format: (1) Colum One: Name of Proposed Arbitrator; (2) Column Two: Objection; (3) Colum Three: Response to Objection. In the alternative, by no later than May 14, 2018 parties may submit a joint statement of name(s) of the agreed upon arbitrator(s) from the lists provided on April 27, 2018 or any other proposed arbitrator (accompanied by CVs and brief regarding qualifications, not to exceed 1 page). =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 22, 2018, Line 4. DEFENDANT STEPHEN HOM'S MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH AMENDED COMPLAINT. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Request for Judicial Notice of the Grant Deed is granted. The following shall be stricken from the Fourth Amended Complaint: (1) "improperly" and "all of which materially breached the terms of the Lease Agreement" from paragraph 42; and (2) "improper" from paragraph 43. Motion is otherwise denied. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: NORMA ANN MULLIGAN VS. LIVERPOOL LIL'S INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, March 22, 2018, Line 5. DEFENDANTS LIVERPOOL LIL'S INC., GILBERT "GIL" HODGES' MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, ETC. DENIED. The renewed motion fails to meet the requirements of CCP 1008(b). Affidavit required under CCP 1008(b) submitted with reply papers fails to explain why it had not been submitted with the moving papers. Additionally, the "cap" on recovery of damages to the premises is at the heart of motion for good faith settlement in this case. Moving party fails to explain why it was a surprise that opposing party and the Court evaluated the definition of "the premises" under the Lease ("2942 Lyon Street") , Insurance Policy ("ALL DESCRIBED LOCATIONS" - no locations described in the two pages of the policy provided to the Court) and Complaint (2940 Lyon and 2942 Lyon Street). The Court notes that the moving party at the original hearing did not indicate that it needed more time to address the Court's concern raised in the tentative ruling. Finally, in the renewed motion the moving party concedes that "the phrase 'All Described Locations' is nowhere defined" and it "would appear" to include all locations of the business, whether they appear in the invoices, correspondence, the policy, or elsewhere. (Reply 5:20-22). No legal authority is cited for this proposition. Moving party does not argue that in the absence of the "cap" the settlement is in good faith per Tech-Built. =(501/REQ)
